cases.title: Critical config change audit record persisted per testing-plan §3 scenario matrix
preconditions: Event in RegistrationOpen state with initial capacity 10
Authenticated OrganizerAdmin actor with event scope
Registration has been opened (critical-change window active per INV-06)
steps: Invoke eventService.update(eventId, { ruleConfig: { capacity: 20 }, reasonCode: "CAPACITY_CHANGE", reasonText: "Expanded seats due to venue update" }, organizerAdminContext)
Query auditService.listAuditLogs(eventId, { entityType: "EventRuleConfig" })
Locate entry with action event.rule_config.updated
cases.expected: Audit record persisted with actorId, actorRole OrganizerAdmin, reasonCode CAPACITY_CHANGE, reasonText, before.capacity 10, after.capacity 20, and occurredAt timestamp; satisfies testing-plan §3 row: Critical config change audit → audit record persisted (AC-11, BR-22)

cases.title: Post-open capacity change writes immutable audit row in audit_logs table
preconditions: Authenticated OrganizerAdmin actor with mandatory reason payload
Real Postgres test database with transaction rollback per test
steps: Invoke eventService.update with ruleConfig capacity 20, reasonCode CAPACITY_CHANGE, and reasonText via event module
Query audit_logs table directly for eventId and action event.rule_config.updated
Verify event module invoked audit module within the same transaction boundary
cases.expected: Exactly one append-only audit_logs row persisted with before/after capacity diff, actorId, actorRole, reasonCode, reasonText, and occurredAt; module-integration boundary per audit module contract

cases.title: Flow C admin critical rule change after registration open is verifiable via audit-logs API
preconditions: E2E event progressed to RegistrationOpen with capacity 10
OrganizerAdmin JWT with scope to the event
steps: PATCH /api/v1/events/{eventId} with ruleConfig capacity 25, reasonCode CAPACITY_CHANGE, reasonText, and Idempotency-Key header
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/audit-logs?entityType=EventRuleConfig with organizer Authorization bearer token
Find item with action event.rule_config.updated
cases.expected: PATCH succeeds; audit list returns HTTP 200 paginated envelope containing entry with matching reasonCode, reasonText, before/after capacity diff, actorId, and occurredAt per testing plan Flow C

cases.title: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/audit-logs returns HTTP 200 paginated envelope
preconditions: RegistrationOpen event with at least one prior critical rule config change audit entry
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/audit-logs?entityType=EventRuleConfig&page=1&pageSize=5 with admin Authorization bearer token
Verify HTTP status code and response envelope shape
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with items array, page, pageSize, total, totalPages; at least one item has action event.rule_config.updated with reasonCode visible per API design §2.5 and §3

cases.title: Organizer audit log page surfaces actor, action, and reason columns with pagination
preconditions: OrganizerAdmin logged in with access to event that has audit log entries
steps: Navigate to organizer event audit log page
Observe table columns for Action, Actor, and Reason
Use pagination controls when total exceeds pageSize
cases.expected: Table renders audit action label, actor identifier, and human-readable reason text per governance UX spec; server-driven pagination controls present and do not render unbounded dataset client-side

cases.title: Non-capacity rule config field change after registration open is audit logged with before/after diff
preconditions: OrganizerAdmin actor with mandatory reason payload
steps: Record current ruleConfig snapshot as beforeState
Update ruleConfig to toggle waitlistEnabled (or adjust registration/check-in window) with reasonCode and reasonText
List audit logs filtered by entityType EventRuleConfig and compare before/after payloads
cases.expected: Audit entry persisted capturing beforeState and afterState for changed rule field, actor identity, and reason metadata; major post-open rule change traceable per BR-22 state-transition guard

cases.title: Critical config change without reason returns AUDIT_REQUIRED_FOR_CRITICAL_CHANGE
steps: PATCH /api/v1/events/{eventId} with ruleConfig capacity change only (no reasonCode or reasonText)
cases.expected: HTTP 422 or 400 with error code AUDIT_REQUIRED_FOR_CRITICAL_CHANGE; no rule config mutation and no audit entry for the failed attempt; transaction rolled back per error-handling policy

cases.title: OrganizerStaff cannot modify event-level rule configuration
preconditions: OrganizerStaff JWT assigned to the event
steps: PATCH /api/v1/events/{eventId} with ruleConfig capacity change and reason fields using staff token
cases.expected: HTTP 403 with EVENT_RULE_CHANGE_FORBIDDEN; no rule config change and no new audit entry for unauthorized actor per roles-permissions §2

cases.title: Participant cannot read event audit logs
preconditions: Participant JWT registered on the event
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/audit-logs with participant Authorization bearer token
cases.expected: HTTP 403 forbidden; audit logs restricted to OrganizerAdmin per permission matrix

cases.title: OrganizerStaff cannot access audit logs endpoint
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/audit-logs with staff Authorization bearer token
cases.expected: HTTP 403 with FORBIDDEN; staff has limited audit read per roles-permissions matrix (Read audit logs: Limited)

cases.title: Unauthenticated GET audit-logs returns 401
preconditions: Event with existing audit log entries
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/audit-logs without bearer token
cases.expected: HTTP 401 unauthenticated; audit data not exposed

cases.title: Audit log page beyond totalPages returns empty items with valid metadata
preconditions: Event with known audit log count
steps: Call listAuditLogs with page=1 pageSize=2 to read total and totalPages
Call listAuditLogs with page exceeding totalPages
cases.expected: Beyond-last-page response has items: [] with matching total and totalPages per pagination contract

cases.title: Invalid pagination on audit-logs returns INVALID_PAGINATION
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/audit-logs?page=0
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/audit-logs?page=1&pageSize=101
cases.expected: HTTP 400 with INVALID_PAGINATION for sub-1 page or pageSize above max 100; no partial audit data returned

cases.title: Sequential critical config changes produce distinct immutable audit entries
steps: Apply first critical update (capacity 15) with reason payload
Apply second critical update (capacity 20) with distinct reason payload
List audit logs for EventRuleConfig entity ordered by occurredAt
cases.expected: Two separate audit entries with monotonic occurredAt ordering; each captures its own before/after diff and reason; configuration version history preserved per BR-22

cases.title: Every successful post-open critical config change has corresponding audit record
preconditions: Harness gate: no missing audit event for critical operations
steps: Perform authorized critical rule config update with reason
Query audit_logs table or listAuditLogs API for matching eventId and action
cases.expected: Exactly one append-only audit record exists per successful critical change; satisfies NFR-10 traceability requirement and testing plan P1 defect model for missing audit

cases.title: Audit record includes minimum payload fields for operational review
preconditions: RegistrationOpen event after successful critical config change
steps: Retrieve audit entry for event.rule_config.updated action
Inspect actorId, actorRole, action, entityType, entityId, eventId, before, after, reasonCode, reasonText, occurredAt
cases.expected: All minimum audit payload fields populated per roles-permissions §6; append-only policy enforced (no mutation endpoint)

cases.title: Audit logs list defaults to createdAt descending order
preconditions: Event with multiple audit entries at different timestamps
steps: List audit logs without explicit sort parameter
Compare occurredAt timestamps across consecutive items
cases.expected: Items ordered newest-first per API design default sort createdAt:desc; supports operational review of latest critical changes
